rationale: Both evaluations reach the same conclusion for the same substantive reason: the candidate's 'Basic' German falls short of the mandatory C1 requirement, treated as a knockout in each. The strengths (technical breadth, open-source commitment, architectural skill) and the other concerns (no formal people-management experience, limited client-facing consulting background) are essentially identical and merely reworded. The one-point score difference (3 vs 4) with no change in reasoning is within ordinary run-to-run noise, and none of the variant's reasoning keys off the swapped employer names, which carry no prestige signal in either direction.
